evals.expected_output: A list of incomplete reminders obtained by running `event reminders list`

assertions.name: Returns valid list
assertions.description: The output file `reminders.md` exists and contains a valid list of reminders, or a message saying no reminders were found.

assertions.name: Only incomplete
assertions.description: The output does not contain any completed reminders (checkboxes with [x]).

evals.prompt: Create a new reminder titled 'Review PR' in the 'Work' list, set priority to High, and mark it as flagged.
evals.expected_output: Creates a reminder using the `event reminders create` command with the appropriate flags.

assertions.name: Created successfully
assertions.description: The output file `creation_result.md` exists and shows a successful creation of a reminder titled 'Review PR'.

assertions.name: List matches
assertions.description: The reminder was created in the 'Work' list.

assertions.name: Flagged status matches
assertions.description: The reminder is marked as flagged.

evals.prompt: What's on my calendar for the next 3 days?
evals.expected_output: A list of calendar events obtained by running `event calendar list` with appropriate date range flags.

assertions.name: Returns valid list
assertions.description: The output file `calendar_events.md` exists and contains a list of calendar events, or a message saying none were found.

evals.prompt: remind me to call the dentist tomorrow at 3pm
evals.expected_output: Interprets the free-form instruction and creates a reminder via `event reminders create` with a title about calling the dentist and a `--due` resolved to tomorrow at 15:00:00.

assertions.name: Created as a reminder
assertions.description: A reminder (not a calendar event) was created via `event reminders create` with a title referencing the dentist call.

assertions.name: Relative date resolved
assertions.description: The `--due` value is an absolute `yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss` timestamp set to the next calendar day at 15:00:00, derived from the current date.

evals.prompt: I just agreed with my manager to deliver the Q3 budget report by this Friday. Now invoke the apple-events skill with no arguments.
evals.expected_output: With no explicit instruction, the skill infers an actionable item from the conversation, classifies it as a task vs event, and asks the user to confirm before creating anything (via AskUserQuestion). It must NOT silently list reminders/events and must NOT create without confirmation.

assertions.name: Captures context instead of listing
assertions.description: The skill identifies the 'deliver Q3 budget report by Friday' commitment from the conversation rather than defaulting to a `event reminders list` / `event calendar list` read.

assertions.name: Asks before creating
assertions.description: The skill asks the user to confirm the inferred item (proposing a reminder/task or a calendar event with a due/start date) and does not create anything in EventKit before getting confirmation.
